channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: PM Modi Criticises Mamata's Government Over Violence, Promises Action on Bomb-Making and Infiltration in Bengal
item.description: In his recent rally in Birbhum, West Bengal, Prime Minister Narendra Modi heavily criticised the Mamata Banerjee-led Trinamool Congress (TMC) government, accusing them of turning bomb-making into a wi...

item.title: 19-year-old man from Hampshire jailed for 28 months for grooming 14-year-old girl met on Roblox
item.description: A 19-year-old man from Eastleigh in Hampshire has been sentenced to 28 months in prison for grooming a 14-year-old girl he met on the online gaming platform Roblox. Carlo Tritta admitted making indece...

item.title: Tanzanian youngster Joseph Jr earns spot in RC Celta de Vigo U19 team
item.description: A young Tanzanian footballer named Joseph Jr Michael Joseph, also known as JJ, is making big steps in European football. Born in 2008, he has joined the U19 B side of Spain’s RC Celta de Vigo after ne...

item.title: Netanyahu orders direct talks with Lebanon for Hezbollah disarmament amid ongoing strikes and global criticism
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu announced on Thursday that he has instructed his cabinet to start direct negotiations with the Lebanese government as soon as possible. The main focus of thes...

item.title: Pakistan, Saudi Arabia, Turkey, and Egypt Forge New Alliance Amid Growing Regional Tensions
item.description: In light of the escalating conflict involving Iran, Pakistan, Saudi Arabia, Turkey, and Egypt have come together to form a new bloc aimed at reshaping the geopolitical landscape of West Asia. This dev...

item.title: Mi’kmaw chiefs in Nova Scotia seek pause on cannabis store raids amid treaty rights dispute
item.description: Mi’kmaw chiefs in Nova Scotia are calling for a temporary halt to RCMP raids on cannabis stores within their communities. The demand comes in the wake of recent First Nations-led highway blockades, wh...

item.title: Melania Trump publicly denies any involvement with Jeffrey Epstein
item.description: First Lady Melania Trump delivered a rare public statement from the White House to categorically deny any connection with Jeffrey Epstein or knowledge of his criminal activities. She condemned what sh...

item.title: Housing Australia Future Fund shows early progress but faces challenges in meeting targets
item.description: The Housing Australia Future Fund (HAFF), established in November 2023, aims to deliver 55,000 new social and affordable homes nationwide by 2029. Despite early activity, progress has been slow and un...

item.title: Government Implements CAPF General Administration Act Following Presidential Approval
item.description: On Thursday, the Government of India officially notified the Central Armed Police Forces (CAPF) Act, 2026, following the assent given by President Droupadi Murmu. This legislation acts as an umbrella ...

item.title: Melania Trump publicly refutes claims of association with Jeffrey Epstein
item.description: U.S. First Lady Melania Trump has publicly denied any personal relationship with disgraced financier and convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein. Speaking from the White House, she labelled the claims ...

item.title: South Africa sees significant growth in call centre jobs, boosting youth employment and foreign revenue
item.description: South Africa’s call centre sector has experienced substantial growth, creating 8,180 new international jobs between April and June 2025, according to a report by Business Process Enabling South Africa...

item.title: IMF Warns of Lower Global Growth and Higher Costs from Ongoing Middle East Conflict
item.description: The International Monetary Fund (IMF) don announce say dem go reduce their global growth forecast for this year because of the war wey dey happen for the Middle East. IMF Managing Director, Kristalina...

item.title: FG Condemns Eze Ndigbo Coronations in Diaspora, Partners Ohanaeze to Stop the Practice
item.description: The Federal Government of Nigeria has come out strongly to condemn the growing trend of crowning Eze Ndigbo in foreign countries, calling it very embarrassing and something wey fit cause serious diplo...

item.title: Vincent's Unwavering Trust in Stella in 'The Alibi' Episode 17
item.description: In Episode 17 of 'The Alibi,' Vincent's relationship with Stella takes center stage as his unwavering trust in her is showcased. Despite the intense challenges surrounding their personal and professio...

item.title: IMF Plans $50 Billion Support for Economies Affected by Middle East Conflict
item.description: The International Monetary Fund (IMF) has announced plans to provide up to $50 billion in immediate financial support to countries affected by the ongoing Middle East war. IMF Managing Director Krista...

item.title: Former Malawi President Launches Commonwealth-Led Dialogue Mission in Tanzania
item.description: Dar es Salaam. Former Malawi President Lazarus Chakwera has officially started his diplomatic visit to Tanzania under the Commonwealth framework, aiming to promote reconciliation and dialogue followin...

item.title: PCO clarifies reason for blurred photo of Marcos meeting amid misinformation
item.description: An official photo released by the Presidential Communications Office (PCO) showing President Ferdinand 'Bongbong' Marcos Jr.'s Unified Package for Livelihoods, Industry, Food and Transport (UPLIFT) me...

item.title: Top-earning female Australian athletes and their commercial success
item.description: The landscape of women’s sport in Australia is evolving rapidly, with higher prize money, improved playing contracts, and increasing commercial opportunities. While stars like Sam Kerr and Mary Fowler...

item.title: Chimpanzee group splits into deadly factions, with 28 fatalities
item.description: For over two decades, researchers observed the Ngogo chimpanzee group in Uganda's Kibale National Park, witnessing a stable community of over 200 members. However, this peaceful existence was shattere...

item.title: Former Malawi President Chakwera Arrives in Tanzania to Support National Reconciliation
item.description: Former Malawi President Lazarus Chakwera has arrived in Dar es Salaam, Tanzania, for an official visit as a Commonwealth Special Envoy. His mission is to facilitate reconciliation and promote dialogue...
